schema_notes: Extended from phase2_claim_ledger_v1: each entry now carries an explicit tier (T1/T2/T3/T4) and kill_test field. Bounded/non-claim entries from prior cycles are retained.

entries.claim_text: FRW expansion and freeze-frame shrinkage are conformally equivalent descriptions of the same observable cosmology, related by σ(t).
entries.kill_test: Mathematical inconsistency in the conformal coordinate transformation. Essentially impossible at the kinematic level.

entries.claim_text: Universal coherent scaling of m, G, r ensures local dimensionless observables (atomic clocks, GPS, lunar laser ranging) exhibit no secular drift.
entries.kill_test: Detection of secular drift in dimensionless local observables not attributable to known systematic. Constrained by Eötvös, MICROSCOPE, atomic-clock comparison limits.

entries.claim_text: A phenomenological σ(t) ansatz reproduces Pantheon+SH0ES, DESI BAO, and fσ8 late-time data within ΔAIC < 4 of ΛCDM.
entries.kill_test: No σ(t) ansatz consistent with the geometric-lock condition (T1) achieves Δχ² < 50 vs ΛCDM on combined late-time data.

entries.claim_text: GSC predicts a calculable Δr_s/r_s shift relative to ΛCDM in DESI Year-3 BAO, computed from σ-evolution of c_s and t_rec.
entries.kill_test: Observed Δr_s/r_s outside the GSC-predicted band at >3σ falsifies T2 for the registered ansatz family.

entries.claim_text: The gravitational coupling G(σ) follows an RG-running profile with a critical scale σ_* near a hadronic length, motivated by Asymptotic Safety scenarios.
entries.kill_test: All G(σ) profiles consistent with T2 phenomenology are excluded by precision tests of equivalence-principle universality, dimensionless-constant variation (Oklo, atomic clocks for α and m_e/m_p), or solar-system Ġ/G bounds; OR a first-principles FRG derivation produces a running incompatible with T2.

entries.claim_text: σ_* is currently a phenomenological parameter; first-principles derivation from FRG, non-commutative IR/UV mixing, or AdS/QCD warp factor is outstanding work.
entries.kill_test: Persists until a first-principles derivation is produced. Two candidate routes are under exploration.

doc_locations.section: 3.2 Status of σ_*; 3.5 Two Candidate Derivations

entries.claim_text: The same RG flow that drives G(σ) running induces a σ-F̃F coupling, providing cosmological θ-relaxation without a separate axion field. Predicts CMB cosmic birefringence consistent with Planck hint and bounded by nEDM measurements.
entries.kill_test: FRG calculation showing the gravitational fixed point produces zero or wrong-sign σ-F̃F coupling; OR LiteBIRD measurement of birefringence amplitude incompatible with late-time σ-fit at >3σ; OR future tighter nEDM bound incompatible with predicted θ_eff trajectory.

entries.claim_text: The σ_*-crossing constitutes a continuous phase transition; topological defects formed at the crossing carry Kibble–Zurek density and produce the dark-matter abundance.
entries.status: candidate derivation; KZ critical exponents pending
entries.kill_test: NANOGrav, EPTA, or LISA exclusion of cosmic-string GW background at the GSC-predicted amplitude; OR LiteBIRD/CMB-S4 B-mode pattern incompatible with predicted defect contribution; OR dwarf-galaxy DM phenomenology incompatible with vortex-tangle predictions.

entries.claim_text: Spatial σ(x,t) field generates MOND-like phenomenology in galactic potentials, with predictions distinct from generic MOND at cluster scale and in voids.
entries.kill_test: DESI peculiar-velocity surveys finding standard ΛCDM consistency at GSC-predicted deviation level; OR cluster mass-temperature relations incompatible with σ(x)-predicted profiles.

entries.claim_text: σ-running tracks cosmological information density; identifying σ̇/σ with holographic complexity rate provides a microphysical origin for σ-evolution. Time emerges as RG-flow parameter (Connes thermal-time hypothesis).
entries.kill_test: Structural inconsistency with established holographic-complexity bounds (Lloyd) or Connes modular flow formalism.

entries.claim_text: GSC admits reformulation as a cosmological quantum reference frame in the Giacomini–Brukner formalism; the geometric-lock condition becomes a theorem about invariance of dimensionless observables under σ-QRF transformations.
entries.kill_test: Mathematical inconsistency between the σ-QRF Hilbert space construction and the established Giacomini–Brukner formalism. (Empirical kill-tests are indirect, via decoherence-rate bounds on σ-superpositions from CMB primary anisotropies.)

doc_locations.section: 8 (T4) σ as Cosmological Quantum Reference Frame

entries.claim_text: The ~9 second beam-trap discrepancy in neutron lifetime measurements is explained by σ-environmental dependence (different σ-equilibria in different matter-density environments).
entries.kill_test: Different trap geometries (varying wall material, density, distance) showing no corresponding τ_n variation at the GSC-predicted level.

entries.claim_text: GW-memory passage induces a permanent shift in σ-equilibrium, observable as correlated frequency shifts in a globally-distributed atomic-clock network following LIGO/Virgo merger events.
entries.status: candidate prediction; σ-GW coupling formalism pending
entries.kill_test: No correlated atomic-clock frequency shift observed in post-merger data at the GSC-predicted amplitude given measured GW-memory amplitudes.

entries.claim_text: Early-time bridge uses compressed CMB priors and does not provide a full TT/TE/EE spectra fit in-repo.
entries.kill_test: N/A — this is an explicit non-claim. Promoting to a full closure claim would require Phase-3+ Boltzmann integration.

entries.claim_text: Structure-formation support is linear-theory diagnostic tooling (transfer/growth/fσ8 overlay), not a full nonlinear LSS closure.
entries.kill_test: N/A — this is an explicit non-claim. Promotion would require nonlinear Boltzmann + N-body integration.

entries.claim_text: Boltzmann export/results paths are reproducibility packaging for external CLASS/CAMB runs and do not compute spectra in-repo.
entries.kill_test: Reproducer cannot regenerate the export bundle deterministically from registered inputs.

entries.claim_text: The framework does not claim dark matter is solved, replaced, or eliminated. The KZ-derived vortex-DM (T4) is a candidate mechanism, not a closure.
entries.kill_test: N/A — explicit non-claim. T4 vortex-DM derivation independently kill-tested under CLM-T4-VORTEX-DM-KZ-008.

entries.claim_text: Open problems explicitly listed: σ_* derivation, σ-F̃F FRG calculation, KZ critical exponents, σ-QRF Hilbert space, full CMB closure, full nonlinear structure closure.
entries.kill_test: N/A — meta-claim about scope. Each open problem has its own kill-test in the corresponding tier entry above.
